slides.title: The longest-lived people eat *mostly plants*.
slides.sub: Across the world's Blue Zones, the plate leans plant-forward. Here is the simple pattern to borrow.

slides.title: Look to the Blue Zones
slides.body: The world's longest-lived communities eat *mostly plants*, with meat as an occasional side, not the center.

slides.title: Fiber feeds your gut
slides.body: Plants bring fiber that feeds a diverse gut microbiome, which *is linked to healthy aging* and steady energy.

slides.title: Beans most days
slides.body: Aim for variety across the week, with beans or lentils most days as *a quiet longevity staple*.

slides.title: Add before you subtract
slides.body: Think additive: crowd the plate with plants rather than only restricting. *Abundance beats deprivation* for sticking with it.

slides.title: Eat the rainbow
slides.body: Different colors carry different protective compounds. Variety across the week *covers more bases* than any single food.

slides.title: Make plants the *base of the plate*.
slides.body: Vibe Health scores your food, water and movement into one daily number and a Health Score you can actually follow.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
The world's longest-lived people share one habit: they eat mostly plants. 🌿

Fiber feeds a diverse gut microbiome linked to healthy aging, beans most days help, and different colors bring different protective compounds. Add plants, don't just restrict. 🔖

Vibe Health turns your plate into one simple daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Eat Like the World's Longest-Lived People
